title: Section 7
content: (1) The 1[Government] shall appoint some officer or body of persons to be conservator of every port subject to this Act. (2) Subject to any direction by the 1[Government] to the contrary,-- (a) in ports where there is a port-officer, the port-officer shall be the conservator; (b) in ports where there is no port-officer, but where there is a harbour-master, the harbour-master shall be the conservator. (3) Where the harbour-master is not conservator, the harbour-master and his assistants shall be subordinate to, and subject to the control of, the conservator. (4) The conservator shall be subject to the control of the 1[Government], or of any intermediate authority which 2[the Government] may appoint.
footnote: 1. Subs. by the A.O. 1937, for "Local Government". 2. Subs., ibid., for "that Government".
